title: The programming languages dying fastest in 2025
post:
Some uncomfortable truths about programming languages in 2025:

PHP is effectively dead outside legacy systems. Ruby barely exists outside Rails apps. Perl is a memory. Go is plateauing.

Meanwhile, TypeScript is eating the world. Rust is growing in systems programming. And Python has become the de facto second language for AI work.

The pattern is clear: languages that don't evolve die. PHP had its moment. Ruby on Rails was revolutionary. But innovation stopped, and developers moved on.

If you're investing your career in a language, check its trajectory, not just its current popularity. A language that's declining is a liability. A language that's growing is leverage.

I'm not saying these languages don't have value. I'm saying your time is finite, and where you invest it matters.

What's your language bet for the next five years? Thoughts?
